title: Sequence Distances, Clustering, and Stability
text: PAM uses supplied dissimilarities directly. CLARA uses documented classical multidimensional-scaling embedding cluster::clara() expects observations rather dissimilarity object.

title: Sequence Data Validation and Preparation
text: Ordered categorical data can contain missing states, duplicated positions, unsorted rows, consecutive repeats, zero durations, unknown states, inconsistent metadata. Silent repair can change analytical object. gp3sequences therefore separates non-modifying audit validation policy-driven preparation.

title: Sequence Data Validation and Preparation
text: example includes unsorted sequence, duplicated position, missing state, consecutive repeat, zero duration, unexpected state, unused factor level. Participant group metadata remain constant within sequence.
code: problem_data <- data.frame(   sequence_id = c("s2", "s1", "s1", "s1", "s1", "s2", "s2", "s2", "s2"),   sequence_order = c(2, 2, 1, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 4),   state = factor(     c("search", "search", "home", "search", NA,       "home", "home", "product", "other"),     levels = c("home", "search", "product", "checkout", "other", "unused")   ),   duration = c(120, 100, 90, 110, 80, 100, 0, 150, 130),   participant_id = c("p2", "p1", "p1", "p1", "p1", "p2", "p2", "p2", "p2"),   group = c("interface_b", "interface_a", "interface_a", "interface_a",             "interface_a", "interface_b", "interface_b", "interface_b",             "interface_b"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  expected_states <- c("home", "search", "product", "checkout") problem_data #>   sequence_id sequence_order   state duration participant_id       group #> 1          s2              2  search      120             p2 interface_b #> 2          s1              2  search      100             p1 interface_a #> 3          s1              1    home       90             p1 interface_a #> 4          s1              2  search      110             p1 interface_a #> 5          s1              3    <NA>       80             p1 interface_a #> 6          s2              1    home      100             p2 interface_b #> 7          s2              2    home        0             p2 interface_b #> 8          s2              3 product      150             p2 interface_b #> 9          s2              4   other      130             p2 interface_b

title: gp3sequences
text: gp3sequences independent R package transparent, reproducible, auditable analysis ordered categorical sequences. designed ordinary long-format data frames restricted Gazepoint exports, eye-tracking data, particular hardware, proprietary software.

title: gp3sequences
text: Version 0.1.0 established neutral data contract contiguous-motif MVP. development version adds auditable advanced sequence methods retaining explicit interpretation boundaries optional specialist-package adapters.

title: gp3sequences
text: Potential applications include: eye-tracking AOI scanpaths; website-navigation customer-journey sequences; behavioural-state task-action sequences; interface-interaction sequences; educational activity sequences; repeated ordered categorical data.

title: gp3sequences
text: core data contract require configurable columns corresponding sequence_id, sequence_order, state. Optional participant, trial, condition, group, timing, duration, weight, metadata columns may also mapped explicitly. minimal input may look like :
code: example_sequences <- data.frame(   sequence_id = rep(c("s1", "s2"), each = 4L),   sequence_order = rep(1:4, times = 2L),   state = c(     "home", "search", "product", "checkout",     "home", "category", "product", "home"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  example_sequences #>   sequence_id sequence_order    state #> 1          s1              1     home #> 2          s1              2   search #> 3          s1              3  product #> 4          s1              4 checkout #> 5          s2              1     home #> 6          s2              2 category #> 7          s2              3  product #> 8          s2              4     home

title: gp3sequences
text: initial public API provides three related functions: audit_sequence_data() reports structured data-quality issues; validate_sequence_data() returns compact validity contract; prepare_sequence_data() applies explicit preprocessing policies creates deterministic canonical sequence data.
code: audit <- audit_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  validation <- validate_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  prepared <- prepare_sequence_data(   example_sequences,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state",   repeated_state_policy = "preserve" )

title: gp3sequences
text: Related functions support deterministic encoding descriptive sequence summaries without assigning substantive meaning states: encode_sequence_data() creates transparent state dictionary; summarise_sequence_states() reports state frequencies durations; summarise_sequence_transitions() reports adjacent transition counts; format_sequence_paths() creates compact ordered path strings.
code: encoded <- encode_sequence_data(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  state_summary <- summarise_sequence_states(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  transition_summary <- summarise_sequence_transitions(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )  paths <- format_sequence_paths(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state" )

title: gp3sequences
text: motif workflow enumerates contiguous state windows retains explicit structural definitions: extract_sequence_ngrams() extracts auditable motif occurrences; summarise_sequence_motifs() reports counts sequence prevalence; filter_sequence_motifs() applies explicit transparent thresholds; format_sequence_motifs() creates stable report-ready tables.
code: motif_occurrences <- extract_sequence_ngrams(   prepared$data,   sequence_id_col = "sequence_id",   order_col = "sequence_order",   state_col = "state",   min_length = 2,   max_length = 4,   overlap = "allow" )  motif_summary <- summarise_sequence_motifs(motif_occurrences)  motif_filter <- filter_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   min_sequences = 2,   min_prevalence = 0.10,   top_n = 20,   ties = "include" )  motif_table <- format_sequence_motifs(   motif_filter,   prevalence = "percent",   digits = 1 )

title: gp3sequences
text: Motif occurrences can summarised plotted without adding analytical dependencies: summarise_sequence_motif_positions() reports structural locations; format_sequence_motif_positions() produces explicit report tables; plot_sequence_motifs() plots prevalence occurrence metrics; plot_sequence_motif_positions() plots occurrence locations.
code: position_summary <- summarise_sequence_motif_positions(   motif_occurrences,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative" )  position_table <- format_sequence_motif_positions(   position_summary,   position_units = "percent",   digits = 1 )  plot_sequence_motifs(   motif_summary,   metric = "sequence_prevalence",   top_n = 10 )  plot_sequence_motif_positions(   motif_occurrences,   position = "centre",   scale = "relative",   top_n = 5 )

title: Bootstrap sequence-cluster stability — bootstrap_sequence_clusters
code: sequences <- data.frame(   sequence_id = rep(c("s1", "s2", "s3", "s4"), each = 4L),   sequence_order = rep(1:4, times = 4L),   state = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "A", "B", "C", "C",             "D", "C", "B", "A", "D", "C", "A", "A"),   group = rep(c("g1", "g2"), each = 8L),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) distance <- compute_sequence_distance(sequences) bootstrap_sequence_clusters(distance, k = 2L, n_boot = 5L, seed = 1L) #> $original #> $assignments #> s1 s2 s3 s4  #>  1  1  2  2  #>  #> $model #>  #> Call: #> (function (d, method = "complete", members = NULL) {    METHODS <- c("ward.D", "single", "complete", "average", "mcquitty",         "median", "centroid", "ward.D2")    if (method == "ward") {        message("The \"ward\" method has been renamed to \"ward.D\"; note new \"ward.D2\"")        method <- "ward.D"    }    i.meth <- pmatch(method, METHODS)    if (is.na(i.meth))         stop("invalid clustering method", paste("", method))    if (i.meth == -1)         stop("ambiguous clustering method", paste("", method))    n <- as.integer(attr(d, "Size"))    if (is.null(n))         stop("invalid dissimilarities")    if (is.na(n) || n > 65536L)         stop("size cannot be NA nor exceed 65536")    if (n < 2)         stop("must have n >= 2 objects to cluster")    len <- as.integer(n * (n - 1)/2)    if (length(d) != len)         (if (length(d) < len)             stop        else warning)("dissimilarities of improper length")    if (is.null(members))         members <- rep(1, n)    else if (length(members) != n)         stop("invalid length of members")    storage.mode(d) <- "double"    hcl <- .Fortran(C_hclust, n = n, len = len, method = as.integer(i.meth),         ia = integer(n), ib = integer(n), crit = double(n), members = as.double(members),         nn = integer(n), disnn = double(n), diss = d)    hcass <- .Fortran(C_hcass2, n = n, ia = hcl$ia, ib = hcl$ib,         order = integer(n), iia = integer(n), iib = integer(n))    structure(list(merge = cbind(hcass$iia[1L:(n - 1)], hcass$iib[1L:(n -         1)]), height = hcl$crit[1L:(n - 1)], order = hcass$order,         labels = attr(d, "Labels"), method = METHODS[i.meth],         call = match.call(), dist.method = attr(d, "method")),         class = "hclust")})(d = structure(c(1, 4, 4, 4, 4, 1), Labels = c("s1", "s2", "s3", "s4"), Size = 4L, call = as.dist.default(m = matrix_distance), class = "dist", Diag = FALSE, Upper = FALSE),     method = "average") #>  #> Cluster method   : average  #> Number of objects: 4  #>  #>  #> $medoids #> [1] "s1" "s3" #>  #> $k #> [1] 2 #>  #> $method #> [1] "hierarchical" #>  #> $linkage #> [1] "average" #>  #> $distance #>    s1 s2 s3 #> s2  1       #> s3  4  4    #> s4  4  4  1 #>  #> $seed #> [1] 1 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gp3_sequence_clustering" "list"                    #>  #> $pairwise_stability #>    s1 s2 s3 s4 #> s1  1  1  1  1 #> s2  1  1  1  1 #> s3  1  1  1  1 #> s4  1  1  1  1 #>  #> $evaluated_counts #>    s1 s2 s3 s4 #> s1  4  2  3  3 #> s2  2  3  2  2 #> s3  3  2  4  3 #> s4  3  2  3  4 #>  #> $iterations #>   iteration n_sampled average_silhouette #> 1         1         3                0.5 #> 2         2         3                0.5 #> 3         3         3                0.5 #> 4         4         3                0.5 #> 5         5         3                0.5 #>  #> $overall #>   n_boot sample_fraction mean_pairwise_stability min_pairwise_stability #> 1      5             0.8                       1                      1 #>  #> $settings #> $settings$k #> [1] 2 #>  #> $settings$method #> [1] "hierarchical" #>  #> $settings$linkage #> [1] "average" #>  #> $settings$n_boot #> [1] 5 #>  #> $settings$sample_fraction #> [1] 0.8 #>  #> $settings$seed #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gp3_sequence_cluster_bootstrap" "list"

title: gp3sequences 0.1.0.9000
text: Added four integrated, user-oriented articles covering getting started, validation preparation, method selection, reproducible synthetic case study. Added complete advanced sequence-analysis roadmap. Added deterministic aligned-position consensus sequences agreement summaries. Added descriptive -group state, transition, length comparisons. Added Levenshtein, LCS, optimal-matching, transition-profile distances. Added hierarchical, PAM, CLARA clustering interfaces, validation metrics, representatives, ensembles, stability resampling. Added transition networks, centrality, communities, higher-order transition models, prediction, sequence-level bootstrap intervals. Added dependency-light categorical HMM mixture-HMM estimation, decoding, summaries, model-comparison tables. Added optional adapters TraMineR, arulesSequences, GrpString, seqHMM, igraph, common gp3tools-style data frames. Added four synthetic workflow articles, deterministic tests, end--end smoke test. Initial public release. Created independent gp3sequences package scaffold. Added MIT licensing package-level documentation. Added initial testthat edition 3 infrastructure. Defined initial neutral scope interpretation boundary. Added initial README pkgdown website configuration. Added multi-platform GitHub Actions R CMD check workflow macOS, Windows, Ubuntu release, Ubuntu devel. Added GitHub Actions pkgdown workflow automated website building deployment. Added audit_sequence_data() validate_sequence_data() structured input diagnostics. Added prepare_sequence_data() explicit missing-state, duplicate-position, repeated-state, duration, unknown-state policies. Added encode_sequence_data() deterministic state dictionaries transparent state codes. Added summarise_sequence_states(), summarise_sequence_transitions(), format_sequence_paths() structural sequence summaries. Added extract_sequence_ngrams() deterministic contiguous motif enumeration explicit overlap handling. Added summarise_sequence_motifs(), filter_sequence_motifs(), format_sequence_motifs() transparent motif prevalence, filtering, reporting. Added summarise_sequence_motif_positions() format_sequence_motif_positions() deterministic structural location summaries. Added dependency-free plot_sequence_motifs() plot_sequence_motif_positions() visualisations plus synthetic contiguous-motif workflow article.
